rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CAL200 petition for mandamus and injunctive relief for violation of the education code
rulings.Calendar Matter: Oakland Unified School Districts Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 19, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT OAKLAND UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT Oakland Unified School Districts Motion To Transfer Venue. The motion is denied. San Francisco venue is proper under CCP 395 and CCP 401 because the Cal. Department of Education is a party to this case and the Attorney General has an office in San Francisco. CCP 394 does not apply because plaintiff is not alleging an injury based on negligence. CCP 393 does not apply because Petitioner's petition is based on a failure to act rather than affirmative conduct. See State Commission in Lunacy v. Welch (1908) 154 Cal. 775, 777. The motion to change venue based on CCP 397 [convenience of witnesses] is premature because there is no answer on file. See Easton v. Superior Crt., (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243, 245; Buran Equipment Co., Inc. v. Sup Crt., (1987) 190 Cal.App.3d 1662, 1665. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: NATHALIE HESNARD VS. LYCEE FRANCAIS DE SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion To Quash Defendant Lycee Francais De San Franciscos Subpoenas To Kaiser Permanente San Rafael Medical Offices And Fran Goldie-Marcus, Lcsw
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 19, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF NATHALIE HESNARD's Motion In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion To Quash Defendant Lycee Francais De San Franciscos Subpoenas To Kaiser Permanente San Rafael Medical Offices And Fran Goldie-Marcus, Lcsw Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Parties should be prepared to address issues of scope of relevance of treatment by providers from 2010 to present and whether an in camera examination should be done. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: DINA SERGEYEVA VS. HEMAS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two; Request For Admissions, Set Two And Three; And Motion To Impose Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 19, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF DINA SERGEYEVA's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two; Request For Admissions, Set Two And Three; And Motion To Impose Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Parties should be prepared to address why the responses to requests for admission wherein a denial is made is not sufficient particularly given the statutory consequences for failing to admit a fact that is later proven at trial. With respect to Set No. 2 request #2, #4, it appears that a response should be given in light of the subsequent denial to request #3. With respect to #6, #7, #8 it is not clear what is being admitted. With respect to #10, #11, #12, #13, #14, it appears that responses need to be made in addition to the objections as it appears that these requests are relevant to the subject matter. With respect to Set no. 3, there are objections but no response to requests for admission #1-10 and it is not clear why these are not relevant to the subject matter as broadly defined under the Code of Civil Procedure. With respect to Set no. 2 of the requests for production, it is unclear why these documents are not relevant to the subject matter for purposes of discovery, subject to any applicable privileges. Parties will also advise the court of the tentative ruling regarding the summary judgment and if it is going to be contested, I will allow the parties to go first to Department 302 and then return to allow further argument based on the ruling on the summary judgment. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: DINA SERGEYEVA VS. HEMAS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 19, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT HEMAS, LLC, GERMAN MOTORS CORPORATION DBA MINI OF Amended Notice of Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment. The motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its burden of production. Defendant fails to establish that the sidewalk is part of the state highway system and that it cannot be liable pursuant to San Francisco Public Works Code sec. 706. In addition, the declaration of Bruce Storrs creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the sidewalk is city property. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
